task_completion.description: Verify that the bookmarks bar contains the four required maritime folders and no personal bookmarks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Chrome bookmarks bar. It should contain exactly four folders: 'AIS & Tracking', 'Metocean Data', 'Port Operations', and 'Emergency & USCG'. There should be no visible bookmarks for Netflix, YouTube, Reddit, Twitter, ESPN, Steam, Hulu, TikTok, Facebook, or Instagram.

task_completion.description: Verify that browsing history for personal domains is deleted while maritime history is kept.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Chrome History page (chrome://history). It should show maritime domains (like marinetraffic.com, noaa.gov, vesselfinder.com) but must not contain any entries for the personal/entertainment domains.

task_completion.description: Verify that the browser is set to automatically open the two specified maritime websites on startup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Chrome Settings under 'On startup'. The option 'Open a specific page or set of pages' should be selected, with exactly 'https://www.marinetraffic.com' and 'https://tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov' listed as the pages.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Intensive wake up throttling' is disabled in Chrome flags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the chrome://flags page. The flag for 'Intensive wake up throttling' should be visible and its dropdown menu must be set to 'Disabled'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a custom site search shortcut for 'imo' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Chrome Settings under 'Search engine' -> 'Manage search engines and site search'. There should be a Site search entry visible with the keyword 'imo' and the URL 'https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels?name=%s'.

task_completion.description: Verify that all site notifications are blocked.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Chrome Settings under 'Privacy and security' -> 'Site settings' -> 'Notifications'. The default behavior option 'Don't allow sites to send notifications' should be selected.

task_completion.description: Verify that the default download directory is set to the Vessel_Manifests folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Chrome Settings under 'Downloads'. The 'Location' field should be explicitly set to '/home/ga/Documents/Vessel_Manifests'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Chrome browser GUI to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Chrome browser interface being actively used to change settings, manage bookmarks, and clear history, rather than terminal windows executing SQLite commands or Python scripts to modify browser databases.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Chrome application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bookmarks, settings, and flags should be configured within the actual Google Chrome application UI, not simulated in a text editor or alternative application.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually edit Chrome configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent manually editing the Chrome 'Preferences' or 'Bookmarks' JSON files in a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Personal domains to delete: netflix.com, youtube.com, reddit.com, twitter.com, espn.com, steampowered.com, hulu.com, tiktok.com, facebook.com, instagram.com. Maritime domains to keep: marinetraffic.com, vesselfinder.com, myshiptracking.com, vesseltracker.com, noaa.gov, weather.gov, aapa-ports.org, cargosmart.ai, bic-code.org, uscg.mil. Expected bookmark folders: 'AIS & Tracking', 'Metocean Data', 'Port Operations', 'Emergency & USCG'.
